channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analysis of the Proposed African Charter on Family, Sovereignty and Values
item.description: Between 2023 and 2025, plenty conferences happen for Entebbe, Uganda wey lead to the drafting of one African Charter on Family, Sovereignty and Values. This conference dey backed by Inter-parliamentar...

item.title: Ricky Stuart questions consistency of NRL refereeing after Raiders loss
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart expressed frustration with NRL refereeing following his team's defeat to the Redcliffe Dolphins. Stuart specifically criticised referee Todd Smart, accusing officia...

item.title: Addressing Ebola Outbreaks in Conflict Zones Requires Tackling Violence and Weak Health Systems
item.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is facing another Ebola outbreak, this time identified as the Bundibugyo ebolavirus. Unlike the Zaire strain, Bundibugyo currently has no approved vaccines o...

item.title: Stanbic Bank expands RISE Mama Lishe programme to Dodoma, trains 320 food vendors with business skills and energy-efficient stoves
item.description: Stanbic Bank Tanzania has expanded its RISE Mama Lishe programme to Dodoma region, where it is empowering 320 food entrepreneurs, mostly women and youth known as Mama Lishe and Baba Lishe. These small...

item.title: Acting AG Blanche signs IRS settlement granting Trump family immunity, raising legal and ethical questions
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche has signed documents that grant President Donald Trump and his family immunity from IRS scrutiny, sparking controversy over the legality and ethics of the set...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay and Tom Hiddleston Withdraw from Celebrity Traitors Due to Scheduling Conflicts
item.description: Gordon Ramsay, the renowned TV chef, withdrew from the upcoming series of Celebrity Traitors at the very last minute, citing scheduling conflicts. The series, currently being filmed in the Scottish Hi...

item.title: Gen Z-led ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ gains massive social media following in India
item.description: A new youth-oriented political group in India, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), has gone viral, amassing nearly 15 million Instagram followers in under a week, surpassing the ruling Bharatiya Janata P...

item.title: A Retrospective on Stephen Colbert’s Most Memorable Late Show Moments
item.description: As CBS’s The Late Show With Stephen Colbert concludes its 11-year run, viewers and critics alike are reflecting on the host’s most remarkable contributions. Taking over from David Letterman, Colbert t...

item.description: Gemma Collins, the reality television personality, has defended her recent involvement in a video with Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson highlighting support for children with special educational...

item.title: Bob Uecker and Bob Gibson's 1964 Team Photo Prank and Its Consequences
item.description: In 1964, during the St. Louis Cardinals team photo session, backup catcher Bob Uecker convinced ace pitcher Bob Gibson to hold hands as a lighthearted prank. Both players smiled for the photograph, ai...

item.title: Government Ramps Up Preparations for 2026 Local Government Elections
item.description: The South African government has intensified efforts to ensure that the 2026 Local Government Elections, scheduled for 4 November, are conducted efficiently and transparently. Cooperative Governance a...

item.title: Supreme Court to Review B.C. Appeal on Mineral Rights and Indigenous Rights Law
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada has agreed to hear British Columbia's appeal regarding a previous ruling that found the province's mineral claims system inconsistent with the United Nations Declaration on...

item.title: Photographic Journey Through the Home Counties of 1950s England
item.description: Hardwicke Knight’s photography offers a captivating glimpse into the Home Counties of England during the 1950s, capturing the post-war era with remarkable clarity and nostalgia. His images document th...

item.title: Matthew attempts to sow doubt in Stella's feelings about Vincent in 'The Alibi' Episode 47

item.title: Trump Comments on U.S. Casualties in Conflict with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump, during a media appearance on a tarmac, spoke about the casualties in the ongoing U.S. conflict with Iran, noting that 13 American service members have died. He compared this fi...

item.title: Trump and Iran Engage in Final Stage of Peace Talks Amid Tensions
item.description: Iran is currently reviewing a US proposal mediated by Pakistan as both nations edge towards a potential peace agreement, despite ongoing threats. US President Donald Trump stated that negotiations are...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Modern Beekeeping with 11,000 Hives and 3,000 Trained Beekeepers
item.description: Tanzania is stepping up efforts to modernize its beekeeping sector during the 2026/2027 financial year. The government plans to distribute 11,000 modern hives and provide training for over 3,000 beeke...

item.title: IMD issues red alert for severe heatwave in Banda, Prayagraj, Chitrakoot and Kaushambi in Uttar Pradesh; Delhi on orange alert with first heatstroke cases reported
item.description: The India Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for four districts in eastern Uttar Pradesh due to severe heatwave conditions. The affected districts are Banda, Prayagraj, Chitrakoot, and K...

item.title: Upper Wawa Dam to Boost Water Supply in Preparation for El Niño
item.description: The Upper Wawa Dam in Rizal is set to contribute additional water supply as the Philippines faces the possibility of an upcoming El Niño, which may result in drier conditions and water shortages in af...

item.title: Deputy President Mashatile speaks at Nepad 25th anniversary business breakfast in Cape Town
item.description: On Thursday, Deputy President Paul Mashatile delivered the keynote address at the Nepad@25 business breakfast held in Cape Town. The event was organised by the African Union Development Agency in coll...
